_meta.description: Single-question ambiguity templates and rules per domain for unresolved high-impact uncertainty.

rules.trigger: query references an entity name that matches 2+ distinct entities in the corpus
query mentions a date or period that could map to multiple fiscal/calendar ranges
examples: Q3 could be calendar Q3 or fiscal Q3.

rules.trigger: query uses a metric name that appears under 2+ different definitions in the corpus
examples: 'Revenue' appears as gross and net in different tables.

rules.trigger: query uses a pronoun (it, that, this) without a clear antecedent in conversation history
corpus contains multiple versions of the same document and query does not specify which
examples: Contract v1 and v2 both exist.

rules.trigger: query references a monetary value without specifying currency and corpus contains multiple currencies
examples: Document has USD and BRL values.
Is this in euros or dollars?

rules.trigger: query is broad enough to match 5+ documents with no narrowing signal
two source passages provide contradictory answers to the same question
query uses a partial name that could match multiple full names in the corpus
examples: 'The Silva contract' — Maria Silva or João Silva?
'Report from ABC' — ABC Ltd or ABC Inc?

rules.trigger: query asks for a time-sensitive value but does not specify period
rules.check: context.timeSensitiveQuery == true AND context.periodRef == null AND context.availablePeriods.count > 1

rules.trigger: query uses an abbreviation that maps to 2+ expansions in the corpus
examples: 'ROI' — Return on Investment or Region of Interest?

rules.trigger: query references a section number that exists in multiple documents
examples: Section 3.2 exists in both the lease and the amendment.

rules.trigger: query asks for a comparison but only specifies one side
examples: 'How does this compare?' — compare to what?
'Is it higher?' — higher than which baseline?

rules.trigger: query uses subjective qualifiers (high, low, significant) without defining a threshold
query references a column name that appears in multiple tables across the corpus
query language differs from document language and could cause translation ambiguity
examples: EN query on PT doc: 'revenue' could be 'receita' or 'faturamento'.

rules.trigger: answer depends on a condition in the document whose status is unknown from context
examples: 'If the contract is renewed' — has it been renewed?
'Subject to board approval' — was it approved?

rules.trigger: query could be answered at individual or aggregate level
examples: 'Average cost' — per unit or per batch?

rules.trigger: query references a document type (report, contract) that matches multiple docs of same type
query references a page number and multiple documents have content on that page
examples: Page 5 exists in all 3 uploaded PDFs.

rules.trigger: query references a calculation or formula name that is defined differently in multiple sheets
examples: 'Net profit' formula differs between Sheet1 and Sheet2.

rules.trigger: yes/no question requires additional context that is not available in the corpus
examples: 'Is this compliant?' — compliant with which regulation?

rules.trigger: query uses relative terms (next, previous, above, below) without a clear anchor point
query can be legitimately answered with multiple different correct values from the corpus
query references a numeric value whose unit is ambiguous (kg vs lb, km vs mi, etc.)
query references a party role (buyer, seller, client) that maps to multiple entities
query asks about status but entity has multiple status fields or status history
corpus contains data at mixed granularity (daily, monthly, yearly) and query doesn't specify
examples: Data available daily and monthly — which?

rules.trigger: query contains negation whose scope is syntactically ambiguous
examples: 'Not all employees received bonuses' — who didn't?

rules.trigger: query could be answered from a chart or a table and values may differ due to rounding
rules.check: context.chartSource.present == true AND context.tableSource.present == true AND context.valuesConflict == true
examples: Should I use the chart or table value?

rules.trigger: query implies a filter (active, current) but does not state it explicitly
query references a location name that maps to multiple places in the corpus

questions.question: Which period should I use for this value?
questions.when: period missing and multiple period candidates

questions.question: Which currency should I report this in?
Which two documents should I compare?
questions.when: compare intent and fewer than two explicit docs

questions.question: What should I use as the baseline document?
Which party should I extract obligations for?
Should I extract from all matching records or only active ones?
Which document version should I summarize?
Do you want only the amendment or the full agreement set?
questions.when: base agreement and amendment both match

questions.question: Which start date should anchor the timeline?
Should I use fiscal dates or calendar dates?
Which compliance framework should I validate against?
Which jurisdiction should I apply for this compliance check?
Which encounter date should I use for this result?
Should I keep the original lab unit or convert it?
Should I use the clause text from the original contract or the amendment?
questions.when: same clause id in multiple docs

questions.question: Which definition of this metric should I apply?
Do you want KPI risk at team level or company level?
Which period cutoff should I apply for this accounting value?
Should I evaluate this on accrual basis or cash basis?
Two sources conflict; which source should be treated as authoritative?
Which document do you mean by that name?
Which table should I use for that column?
questions.when: column name appears in multiple tables

questions.question: Which expansion of this abbreviation should I use?
Which status type do you want: operational, approval, or payment?

finance: Which fiscal period are you referring to — {{candidatePeriods}}?
The term '{{metricName}}' has {{definitionCount}} definitions in this document. Which do you mean: {{definitionList}}?
I found {{currencyCount}} currencies ({{currencyList}}). Which currency should I use?
There are {{entityCount}} entities named '{{entityName}}'. Which one: {{entityList}}?
Revenue figures differ between {{sourceA}} and {{sourceB}}. Which source should I use?
Should I report at the consolidated or subsidiary level?
This value appears as both gross and net. Which do you need?
Multiple cost centers match '{{ref}}'. Please specify: {{candidateList}}.
legal: Section {{sectionNum}} appears in {{docCount}} documents. Which document: {{docList}}?
The term '{{partyRole}}' maps to {{partyCount}} parties. Which party: {{partyList}}?
I found {{versionCount}} versions of this contract. Which version: {{versionList}}?
This clause has a condition ('{{condition}}'). Has that condition been met?
The term '{{legalTerm}}' is defined differently in {{defCount}} clauses. Which definition applies?
Multiple effective dates found: {{dateList}}. Which applies to your question?
Which jurisdiction's interpretation should I apply?
medical: The abbreviation '{{abbrev}}' could mean {{expansionList}}. Which one?
Patient data exists for {{periodCount}} time periods. Which period: {{periodList}}?
Dosage is recorded in {{unitList}}. Which unit should I report?
I found results from {{labCount}} different lab panels. Which lab panel?
Multiple diagnoses use code {{code}}. Which condition specifically?
Should I include readings from all visits or the most recent only?
The protocol has {{versionCount}} versions. Which version applies?
ops: There are {{matchCount}} items matching '{{itemRef}}'. Please specify: {{itemList}}.
Data is available at {{granularityList}} granularity. Which level?
This metric is tracked in {{systemCount}} systems with different values. Which system?
Should I include archived records or only active ones?
Status could refer to {{statusTypes}}. Which status type?
Threshold not specified — what value defines '{{qualifier}}'?
